claims.text: Built and open-sourced innerlens, a hallucination-detection runtime on Anthropic's Jacobian-lens interpretability research: reads a model's own late-layer activations per generated token and scores internal support for what it emitted, in a single forward pass with no second model or retrieval; shipped as a drop-in OpenAI-compatible server on PyPI within a day of the research release
claims.confidence: public on GitHub and PyPI, Apache-2.0

claims.text: Validated the innerlens hallucination signal on TriviaQA (200 questions, Qwen3.5-4B, H100): internal confidence predicts answer correctness at AUROC 0.80 through the shipped library's own scoring; internal support collapses from 0.99 on known facts to 0.19 on forced fabrications
claims.confidence: reproducible from eval script in the public repo

claims.text: Fine-tuned Qwen3-8B into a K-12 math tutor with QLoRA across two training rounds (612 curated examples, NEFTune); published the model on Hugging Face
claims.confidence: published model and methodology, publicly verifiable

claims.text: Built an LLM-as-judge evaluation pipeline (Claude Sonnet 4, 50 held-out prompts, 5 criteria); found and corrected judge bias by splitting metrics by domain; fine-tune beat base on all 5 metrics

Built production LLM serving: 4-bit AWQ-quantized domain model self-hosted on vLLM behind an OpenAI-compatible API with three-tier provider failover; benchmarked on a 24 GB GPU at 2-3x faster generation and 70-75% less memory
claims.confidence: self-reported, measured internally at Nextbase Solutions

claims.text: Raised average engagement time per active user by 122% (11m 32s to 25m 35s in three weeks, Firebase Analytics) via a domain-tuned conversational model with persona-conditioned prompts

Built generative media tools in production: SVDQuant INT4-quantized Flux image generation, Wan 2.1/2.2 14B video generation with step-distilled LoRAs and RIFE interpolation, RealESRGAN upscaling, BRIA RMBG-2.0; Dockerized ComfyUI on RunPod serverless GPUs; tools account for over 30% of company revenue
claims.confidence: self-reported, production systems at Nextbase Solutions

claims.text: Co-built a multi-provider LLM gateway (Node.js/TypeScript, 11 providers) with per-key concurrency limits, sticky prompt-cache routing, and gateway-side context compression cutting tool-output tokens by 50-74%

Benchmarked an agent memory-retrieval engine across 6 versions (20-query, 8-category benchmark over a 129K-token corpus): recall 40% to 85%, context tokens down 78-93%; ran ablations showing hashed vectors matched transformer embeddings at 30x faster indexing; contributed the subject-coverage confidence fix (80% to 85%)
claims.confidence: engine built by a colleague; benchmark, ablations, and fix are Yash's; code private

claims.text: Designed and built a competitive-intelligence agent system on the OpenClaw framework: three scheduled agents, file-based memory, per-fact source citations, confidence scores, human verification gate; runs at about $23/month
claims.confidence: self-reported, code private, walkthrough on request

claims.text: Deployed AI agents and RAG pipelines (LangChain, LlamaIndex) for three departments, including a four-phase Gemini video-production planning agent and a Qdrant-based market-intelligence agent
claims.confidence: self-reported, production systems at Nextbase Solutions

claims.text: Built the vision control core of an autonomous diamond-polishing robot: mesh registration, per-facet pose estimation, corrections streamed to a PLC via a pybind11 C++/Python bridge; earlier automated 3D reconstruction from camera and laser scans to 5-micron error with DBSCAN and RANSAC feature extraction
claims.confidence: self-reported, prior role at Techno Smart Diamond Solution

claims.text: This portfolio trains a character-level GRU (hand-written, no ML framework) in the visitor's browser on his own writing, and its fit reports are generated against this claims file with mandatory disclosed gaps

gaps.text: No multi-node pretraining or large-scale RLHF experience; fine-tuning experience is single-GPU QLoRA scale
No production Kubernetes experience; container orchestration experience is Docker plus serverless GPU platforms
About two years of professional AI engineering experience; not a staff-level or team-lead background yet
No peer-reviewed publications; evaluation work is engineering-grade, not academic
Experience is at startups and small product companies, not large-scale FAANG infrastructure
